content.problemStatement: Problem StatementWrite a program that accepts sets of three numbers, and prints the second-maximum number among the three.
constraints: The three integers in a single triplet are all distinct
content.inputFormat: The first line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains three space-separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the second-maximum integer among the three on a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first triplet (1, 2, 3), the second-maximum number is 2. For the second triplet (10, 15, 5), the second-maximum number is 10. For the third triplet (100, 999, 500), the second-maximum number is 500.
For the first triplet (4, 5, 6), the second-maximum number is 5. For the second triplet (7, 8, 9), the second-maximum number is 8.
For the triplet (1, 2, 3), the second-maximum number is 2.

hidden.label: uniform/edge case
hidden.expectedOutput: Error: The three integers in a single triplet are all distinct

solution.approach: The key insight is to sort the three numbers and then print the second number. This works because sorting arranges the numbers in ascending order, so the second number will be the second-maximum. The time complexity is O(1) per test case because we are only sorting three numbers, and the space complexity is O(1) because we are not using any additional space that scales with the input size.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the dominant structure is the array of three integers
